label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about the Hollow Flashlight invented by Ann Makosinski. The passage says that the flashlight has no moving parts or batteries. Then there's a part where Ann wrote in her project that the flashlights don't use batteries, harmful chemicals, or kinetic energy. They don't create noise and will always work. The only limitation is needing a 5°C temperature difference. So looking at the options: A) Safe, noiseless, energy-saving. The passage mentions no harmful chemicals and no noise, so safe and noiseless. Since it uses body heat and no batteries, it's energy-saving. So A might be correct. B) Created by Ann and her friend. The passage says Ann invented it, and the friend in the Philippines had issues with studying due to lack of electricity. So B is probably wrong. C) Will come to market soon. The passage doesn't mention anything about it being on the market. It just talks about her winning the prize, so C is not supported. D) Works only at low temperatures. The passage says it needs a 5°C temperature difference. It doesn't say the ambient temperature has to be low. For example,
